title: cassia brewsteri
intro: Cassia brewsteri, also known as Senna brewsteri, is a flowering plant in the legume family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anthraquinone content, which acts as a laxative. Cassia brewsteri has been used to treat various health issues, including constipation, diarrhea, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. It can be prepared into herbal remedies such as tea, capsules, and tinctures, which should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
